alternatives_considered: Make bucket public — violates private storage design from foundation, exposes all project photos
Proxy route (/api/photos/:id) — adds server load for every image request, slower than direct CDN
Long-lived signed URLs (1 hour) — risk of URL sharing/leaking
factory_decisions.rationale: 60s signed URLs generated server-side in RSC. Photos are only viewable while the page is fresh. Short expiry limits exposure if a URL is shared. Batch generation via createSignedUrls keeps the query count at 1 regardless of photo count.

factory_decisions.decision: Search Before Building audit for document-storage story
factory_decisions.context: Pre-implementation code reuse analysis
factory_decisions.rationale: Found: 0 BUILD (all adapted from existing), 3 EXTEND (server actions, component, pages), 2 REUSE (buildStoragePath pattern, formatFileSize). Key reuse: photo-storage patterns (operations.ts, server actions, component structure) directly adapted for documents. Same Supabase Storage pattern, same project ownership verification, same signed URL approach.

factory_decisions.decision: Documents render as rows (list) not thumbnails (grid) unlike photos
factory_decisions.context: AC-DS-2 says each document renders as a row with name, icon, date, download button. Photos use a grid of image thumbnails. Documents are non-visual files — a list layout is more appropriate.
alternatives_considered: Grid layout like photos — wastes space since documents have no visual preview
Table layout — too formal for a mobile-first construction app
Card layout — each document as a card with icon and metadata
factory_decisions.rationale: AC-DS-2 explicitly says "row" — a compact list with icon + name + date + download button matches the spec and the mobile-first interaction pattern. Each row is a flex container with icon, truncated filename, date/size metadata, download button, and delete button.

factory_decisions.decision: TaskDocumentsManager is entity-agnostic (works for both tasks and stages)
factory_decisions.context: AC-DS-1 says documents can be attached from task OR stage detail screen. Options: two components, one component with entity type prop.
alternatives_considered: Separate TaskDocumentsManager and StageDocumentsManager — duplicates 95% of the code
Single component with entityId + entityType props — one source of truth, test once
factory_decisions.rationale: The upload logic only differs in which FormData field (taskId vs stageId) is populated. A single component with entityType prop eliminates duplication. The stage detail page passes entityType="stage" and the task detail page passes entityType="task".

factory_decisions.decision: Stage documents query filters task_id IS NULL to avoid showing task-level docs at stage level
factory_decisions.context: A document attached to a task within a stage has both task_id and stage_id set. On the stage detail page, we only want stage-level documents (no task_id), not documents attached to individual tasks.
alternatives_considered: Show all documents with this stage_id including task-level ones — confusing, user already sees them on each task page
Filter by stage_id AND task_id IS NULL — clean separation of scope
factory_decisions.rationale: Stage-level documents (e.g., stage permits, stage completion certificates) are conceptually different from task-level documents (e.g., material specs, inspection reports). The IS NULL filter keeps the scope clear.

factory_decisions.decision: Zero-dependency ZIP builder using STORE method for CSV bundle
factory_decisions.context: AC-EX-3 requires a .zip archive. No zip library in package.json. Options: add jszip (~45KB), build a minimal zip encoder, or download CSVs individually.
alternatives_considered: Add jszip dependency — another runtime dependency for a single export feature
Download 4 separate CSV files — violates AC-EX-3 which specifies a single .zip archive
Use server-side zip generation — adds server compute cost and a new API route
factory_decisions.rationale: ZIP STORE format (uncompressed) is a well-documented binary format. The implementation is ~100 lines of pure TypeScript using Uint8Array and TextEncoder — no external deps, fully unit-tested (CRC-32, file headers, EOCD record). CSVs are small text files where compression adds minimal value. Zero-dep = zero supply chain risk for a feature that runs client-side.

factory_decisions.decision: PDF export via window.print() instead of html2canvas + jspdf
factory_decisions.context: AC-EX-4 asks for a downloadable PDF of the Gantt chart. The Gantt is a DOM-based component (divs + SVG arrows), not a canvas.
alternatives_considered: Add html2canvas + jspdf (~700KB combined) — capture DOM to canvas then to PDF. Heavy deps, known issues with SVG rendering, adds significant bundle size.
Use puppeteer/playwright server-side screenshot — requires a headless browser runtime on the server, not available in Vercel Functions.
Use @react-pdf/renderer — requires rewriting the Gantt as React-PDF components, massive scope creep.
factory_decisions.rationale: window.print() is zero-dep, produces high-quality PDF via the browser engine, handles SVG arrows correctly, and works on all target platforms (Chrome, Safari). The user experience is familiar (print dialog → Save as PDF). Logged as a divergence since it opens a dialog rather than auto-downloading.

factory_decisions.decision: Export data fetched via server action, formatted client-side
factory_decisions.context: The export flow needs authenticated data access + file generation. Options: server generates the file and returns it, or server returns raw data and client formats.
alternatives_considered: Server-side file generation via API route returning a Blob — requires streaming the response, content-type headers, and a dedicated route per format
Client-side fetch + format — one server action returns all data, client builds JSON/CSV/ZIP using pure functions
factory_decisions.rationale: One fetchExportData server action returns the complete ExportPayload. Client-side pure functions (buildJsonExport, buildStagesCsv, buildZip) format the data and trigger download via createObjectURL. This keeps the server action simple (data only) and makes the formatting logic fully unit-testable without mocking server infrastructure.

factory_decisions.decision: Search Before Building audit for fix-photos-next-image + fix-server-mime-validation
factory_decisions.context: Pre-implementation code reuse analysis
factory_decisions.rationale: Found: 0 BUILD, 3 EXTEND (photos/page.tsx, TaskPhotosManager.tsx, app/actions/photos.ts), 1 REUSE (lib/photos/operations.ts validatePhotoFile already exists and tested), 0 REFACTOR-THEN-BUILD. Key reuse: validatePhotoFile already existed as a pure function with full test coverage — just imported it into the server action.

factory_decisions.decision: Use fill prop with relative container instead of explicit width/height for next/image
factory_decisions.context: User-uploaded construction photos have unknown dimensions. next/image requires either width+height or fill.
alternatives_considered: Set explicit width={300} height={300} — forces a fixed layout that may not match the aspect-square container, adds CLS risk if actual images differ
Use unoptimized prop — loses all image optimization benefits (srcset, lazy loading, format conversion)
Use fill with relative parent (chosen) — image fills the aspect-square container, object-cover crops to fit, no CLS
factory_decisions.rationale: fill + aspect-square parent + object-cover preserves the exact same visual layout as the original raw while enabling srcset optimization. The parent container defines the aspect ratio, the image fills it. This is the documented Next.js pattern for images with unknown intrinsic dimensions.

factory_decisions.decision: Supabase remotePatterns uses wildcard hostname *.supabase.co for portability
factory_decisions.context: Project uses ***REMOVED***.supabase.co. Could hardcode the project ref or use a wildcard.
alternatives_considered: Hardcode project ref hostname — breaks if project changes or a second project is added
Use process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL to derive hostname at build time — requires dynamic next.config.ts with env parsing
Use *.supabase.co wildcard (chosen) — covers any Supabase project, still restricted to /storage/v1/** path
factory_decisions.rationale: Wildcard hostname is safe because the pathname restriction /storage/v1/** limits to storage-only URLs. This is more portable across environments (dev, staging, prod may use different Supabase projects) without requiring env variable parsing in config.

factory_questions.question: AI Assist chat currently uses a keyword-matched suggester stub. A follow-up story should add a real LLM backend (Claude via Anthropic SDK or OpenAI) behind the existing suggestFromMessage(message, snapshot) interface. Need: decision on model (Claude Haiku for cost vs Sonnet for quality), ANTHROPIC_API_KEY provisioning in Vercel env vars, server-action wrapper with streaming, and a structured-output prompt that returns the AssistantSuggestion shape (title, rationale, changes[]).
factory_questions.resolved_as: Stubbed with deterministic ICF/insulation/solar rules for this cycle; swap to real LLM in a dedicated llm-integration story

factory_questions.question: Broken-dependency warning (AC-TC-2) is currently dormant because template-apply does not write task_dependencies rows. The task-management cycle needs to let users author dependencies between tasks before this UI flag will actually fire. UI + logic + tests are already wired correctly.
factory_questions.resolved_as: Dormant warning — activates automatically once task_dependencies rows exist for the project

factory_questions.question: Supabase Auth email confirmation: Vercel production URL must be added to Supabase allowed redirect URLs before signup emails will work.

cascade_task_dates() does not currently write to shift_alerts. The cascade function updates task dates and material order_by_dates but does not log the changes to shift_alerts. This must be added when implementing the daily-briefing feature area.
factory_questions.resolved_as: Deferred to daily-briefing feature cycle (shift-alerts story)

factory_questions.question: Duration-edit cascade (AC-TM-5) is exercised via cascade_task_dates() RPC — the pure date math is unit-tested but the SQL function itself is not hit from Node tests. A Supabase integration smoke test (seed a 3-task dep chain, bump the middle one's duration, assert downstream dates shifted) would close the loop.
factory_questions.resolved_as: Unit tests cover the pure layer; QA phase should verify the RPC cascade against the live DB

factory_questions.question: Task edit UI only surfaces duration_days. Name, notes, trade, and depends_on editing for EXISTING tasks is out of scope for task-management (the spec only calls out duration edits in AC-TM-5) — those edits will want a dedicated /tasks/:id edit screen in a later story.
factory_questions.resolved_as: Deferred — current scope is create + duration-edit + delete. A future task-edit story can extend StageTasksManager or use the /tasks/:id route.

factory_questions.question: Trade edit allows editing every field (name, specialty, phone, email, notes) but AC-TR-1 only specifies the create contract. Editing is implied by J2-briefing ('call the trade') but not explicitly required. If editing is ever restricted (e.g. locking name once assigned), the UI in TradesManager needs to react.
factory_questions.found_in: AC-TR-1 vs the broader trade-management implied by AC-TR-2/3/4
factory_questions.resolved_as: Full CRUD shipped — matching user expectations for a contacts list. Can be narrowed later if a concrete edit-restriction requirement surfaces.

factory_questions.question: /materials index page (app/(app)/materials/page.tsx) is still the seeded placeholder with pulse skeletons. No AC in material-line-items specifies its content. A follow-up story should define whether it's an ordering queue, a delivery schedule, or an inventory dashboard.
factory_questions.found_in: spec — affected_screens includes /materials but no AC covers it
factory_questions.resolved_as: Deferred — left as placeholder; this story only ships the per-task materials UI on /tasks/:id per AC-ML-1 wording

factory_questions.question: Currency for estimated_cost is hard-coded to EUR in formatCurrency. No project-level currency field exists in the projects schema; vision.json doesn't specify a currency.
factory_questions.resolved_as: Hard-coded EUR for now, matching the Irish construction context from seed_spec/brainstorming. A future story should add projects.currency and thread it through Material displays.

factory_questions.question: AC-DL-4 offline delay-logging: service worker is registered but has no offline action queue. A future story should add IndexedDB-backed mutation replay (Background Sync API) so delays logged offline sync when connectivity returns with the 'Delay synced' toast. Current implementation uses a Server Action over fetch — it will fail visibly with an inline error message when offline rather than queueing.
factory_questions.resolved_as: Deferred — logged inline error on offline for now; documented as env_limitation

factory_questions.question: Log Delay entry point on /briefing (AC-DL-1 says '/tasks/:id or /briefing'): satisfied by the /tasks/:id path for this story. /briefing still shows Upcoming Orders only — when the briefing page grows a Today's Tasks section it should link to the per-task delay picker (or embed one inline).
factory_questions.resolved_as: Deferred — OR satisfied by /tasks/:id. /briefing Log Delay button queued for a daily-briefing follow-up story.

factory_questions.question: Native pull-to-refresh gesture (AC-DB-5 mobile) not implemented — Refresh button covers the data reload. Should a touch-gesture pull-to-refresh be added in a follow-up story?
factory_questions.resolved_as: Satisfied via Refresh button on both mobile and desktop. Native gesture deferred — requires touch event handling or a third-party library.

factory_questions.question: Add Note quick action (AC-QA-3) links to /tasks/:id#notes but no notes editing section exists on the task detail page yet. The task model has a notes field and createTask accepts notes, but there is no inline edit-notes UI on /tasks/:id. A future story should add an editable notes section.
factory_questions.resolved_as: Link to /tasks/:id#notes — the notes editing section will be added in a future task-edit story. The quick action is functional as navigation.

factory_questions.question: Trigger task is included in shift alerts — should it be excluded since the user initiated the change?
factory_questions.found_in: AC-SA-1: "cascades have occurred since the user's last /briefing visit" — ambiguous whether the trigger task counts as a cascade change
factory_questions.resolved_as: Include the trigger. The user may delay task A on /tasks/A, then visit /briefing later and need a reminder that A moved. Excluding it would make the alert list incomplete for multi-device or multi-session workflows.

factory_questions.question: ensureNotificationPreferences lazy-creates on /settings visit, but users who never visit /settings will be excluded from cron push notifications (INNER JOIN). Consider also creating the row on first push subscription (AC-PN-2 flow) or changing the cron to LEFT JOIN.
factory_questions.resolved_as: Accepted for now — users who enable push (AC-PN-2) will visit /settings to do so, triggering the upsert. Edge case: if a future flow enables push from a different page, that page must also call ensureNotificationPreferences.

factory_questions.question: /stages/:id page does not yet show a photos section. The affected_screens for photo-storage includes /stages/:id but only /tasks/:id has the upload UI. A future story could add a StagePhotosManager to /stages/:id that aggregates photos from all tasks in that stage.
factory_questions.resolved_as: Deferred — /photos gallery already groups by stage, and /tasks/:id is the primary upload surface. Per-stage photo section can be added when stage detail pages get richer.

divergences.actually_did: Custom hand-written service worker at public/sw.js
divergences.rationale: next-pwa v5 is webpack-only and incompatible with Next.js 16 Turbopack (default bundler). Custom service worker is actually simpler, has no Workbox dependency, and is fully portable. Push and notification handlers work identically.

divergences.actually_did: proxy.ts with proxy() function and proxyConfig export
divergences.rationale: Next.js 16 renamed middleware.ts to proxy.ts. Confirmed by Vercel platform context. The ƒ Proxy (Middleware) entry in the build output confirms it is correctly detected.

divergences.spec_says: AI assist chat panel should return suggestions for free-form descriptions (e.g. 'I'm doing ICF foundation not traditional pour')
divergences.actually_did: Implemented a deterministic keyword-matched suggester (ICF, insulation, solar) behind the same suggestFromMessage(message, snapshot) interface a real LLM call would use
divergences.rationale: The project has no Anthropic SDK, OpenAI SDK, or API key wiring — shipping a real LLM call would require Vercel env-var plumbing, secret handling, streaming handling, and error/retry logic that belongs in its own story. The rule-based stub demonstrates the accept/reject flow end-to-end for the three seeded template archetypes and can be swapped for a real Claude API call later by replacing the body of suggestFromMessage. This is documented as an env_limitation on the story result.

divergences.spec_says: AC-DB-5: pull down to refresh (mobile) or clicks refresh (desktop)
divergences.actually_did: Refresh button on both mobile and desktop. No native pull-down-to-refresh gesture.
divergences.rationale: Native pull-to-refresh requires either a touch event handler with rubber-band physics or a third-party library. The Refresh button using router.refresh() + useTransition provides the same data reload capability on both form factors. The native gesture can be added in a follow-up without changing the data flow.

divergences.spec_says: AC-DB-2: selectTodayTasks filters to status IN (not_started, in_progress)
divergences.actually_did: selectTodayTasks now also includes status=complete tasks within the date range
divergences.rationale: AC-QA-1/AC-QA-2 require toggling completion on the briefing and undoing it. If complete tasks were excluded, checking a task would remove it from the list instantly, preventing undo. Complete tasks sort after incomplete ones to keep the active work prominent.

divergences.spec_says: AC-EX-4: a PDF snapshot of the current Gantt chart (full project zoom) is rendered and downloaded
divergences.actually_did: PDF export triggers window.print() which opens the browser print dialog. The user can select Save as PDF to download. No automatic PDF file generation.
divergences.rationale: Generating a true PDF programmatically would require adding html2canvas (~400KB) + jspdf (~300KB) — two new dependencies for a single feature. The browser print dialog produces higher-quality output with native PDF rendering. McKinley principle: spend innovation tokens on the core differentiator (export data portability via JSON/CSV), use boring technology (browser print) for the visual snapshot. The Gantt is DOM-based, not canvas-based, so print CSS can style it appropriately.
